skill.name: meeting-minutes
skill.description: Structures medical meeting transcripts into formal minutes with automated action item extraction and decision logging.
skill.summary: Convert meeting transcripts into structured minutes with automated action item and decision extraction.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 41 patterns across 4 files (576 lines), computing a risk score of 100/100. After manual evaluation, all 41 findings are false positives: the 25 'Ruby/shell backtick' detections are markdown inline-code formatting in SKILL.md, not command execution; the 6 'weak cryptographic algorithm' matches are spurious text matches against benign documentation content (e.g., the string 'md' in filenames); the 7 'system reconnaissance' patterns are false matches on audit metadata JSON field names; the 2 'path traversal' mentions appear inside a security checklist warning against traversal (not performing it); and the 2 'hardcoded URL' detections are a single benign source-attribution link to the GitHub repository. The packaged Python script (scripts/main.py) performs only regex-based text extraction with no network, filesystem, or shell execution capabilities. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Source Attribution URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 7 contains a hardcoded URL linking to the source GitHub repository. This is benign attribution metadata with no security impact, noted for completeness.

content.user_title: Generate Structured Meeting Minutes from Transcripts
content.value_statement: Medical meeting transcripts are often unstructured and hard to act on. This skill extracts action items and decisions, then formats them into clear, compliant minutes ready for review and distribution.
actual_capabilities: Extract action items from meeting transcripts using regex pattern matching
Identify and log decisions and resolutions made during meetings
Format raw transcripts into structured minutes with summary sections
Support clinical, research, and administrative meeting type variations
Output structured JSON with minutes, action items, decisions, and meeting metadata
Limit extracted items to the top 10 action items and decisions for concise, focused output
limitations: Does not perform speech-to-text conversion; requires pre-transcribed text input
Action item extraction uses regex patterns, not machine learning or NLP models
Does not integrate with calendar, task management, or scheduling systems
Output accuracy depends on transcript quality, speaker labeling, and structural clarity

use_cases.title: Clinical Trial Meeting Documentation
use_cases.description: Convert clinical research meeting transcripts into FDA and ICH E6 compliant minutes with tracked decisions and action items.

use_cases.title: Team Standup and Sprint Review Notes
use_cases.description: Extract action items and decisions from daily standup or sprint review transcripts for project tracking and follow-up.

use_cases.title: Medical Advisory Board Summaries
use_cases.description: Structure advisory board discussion transcripts into formal minutes suitable for regulatory submission and stakeholder distribution.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Transcript Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this meeting transcript into structured minutes. Extract all action items and decisions: [paste transcript here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick conversion of a short meeting transcript into minutes

prompt_templates.title: Clinical Meeting with Compliance Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this clinical trial meeting transcript into formal minutes following ICH E6 guidelines. Meeting type is clinical. Extract action items, decisions, and identify all attendees mentioned: [paste transcript here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Formal clinical trial meeting requiring regulatory compliance documentation

prompt_templates.title: Research Team Meeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Structure this research team meeting transcript into minutes. Meeting type is research. Focus on methodological decisions and protocol-related action items: [paste transcript here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Research team meeting discussing study protocols and methodology

prompt_templates.title: Administrative Meeting with Full Tracking
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate comprehensive meeting minutes from this administrative meeting transcript. Include a summary, all decisions, action items with responsible parties and deadlines, and a list of attendees. Meeting type is admin: [paste transcript here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Full administrative meeting documentation with detailed item tracking

output_examples.input: We held a clinical review meeting. Dr. Chen reported that patient enrollment is behind schedule. The team decided to extend the enrollment period by 4 weeks. Action item: Dr. Smith will update the protocol by Friday.
output: Summary: Clinical review meeting covering enrollment status and protocol updates
Decisions: Extended enrollment period by 4 weeks
Action Items: Dr. Smith will update the protocol by Friday

output_examples.input: The research team met to discuss preliminary results. Dr. Jones agreed to revise the statistical analysis plan. Action item: lab team needs to validate the assay by next Wednesday. The group resolved to submit the interim report to the IRB.
output: Summary: Research team discussing preliminary results and analysis revisions
Decisions: Submit interim report to the IRB
Action Items: Dr. Jones to revise statistical analysis plan / Lab team to validate assay by next Wednesday

best_practices: Verify the transcript is complete and includes speaker labels before starting extraction
Review all extracted action items and decisions against the source transcript for accuracy
Specify the meeting type to improve regex pattern matching relevance for domain-specific terms
anti_patterns: Using this skill for non-meeting content such as interviews, lectures, or general conversations
Expecting perfect extraction without reviewing the output for missing or incorrectly matched items
Providing transcripts that lack clear speaker labels, section markers, or structured dialogue formatting

faq.question: What types of meetings does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports clinical, research, and administrative meeting types. Each type adjusts the output format and extraction patterns accordingly.

faq.question: Does this skill perform speech-to-text conversion?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires a pre-transcribed text input. It does not process audio or video files.

faq.question: How accurate is the action item extraction?
faq.answer: Extraction uses regex pattern matching and works best with well-structured transcripts containing clear action language such as 'action item', 'will', 'should', 'need to', and 'must'.

faq.question: Can this skill handle very long meeting transcripts?
faq.answer: Yes, but the output is limited to the top 10 action items and top 10 decisions. Very long transcripts should be chunked or pre-summarized for best results.

faq.question: What compliance standards does this skill follow?
faq.answer: The skill references ICH E6 R2 guidelines and FDA meeting documentation standards for clinical meeting minutes, with GCP compliance considerations.

faq.question: Can I customize the output format?
faq.answer: The default output is structured JSON with minutes text, action items, decisions, and meeting type. You can request format adjustments in your prompt for specific needs.
